skill.name: maxhub-youtube
skill.description: YouTube 全场景数据查询助手。支持Web/V2双版本API，覆盖视频详情、频道数据、搜索、评论、字幕、Shorts等全功能。
skill.summary: Query YouTube data including video details, channel stats, search results, comments, subtitles, and Shorts via the MaxHub API platform.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate YouTube data API client that uses curl commands to query the MaxHub API service. The 815 static findings are overwhelmingly false positives from documentation code examples in markdown files. The skill legitimately requires environment variable access (MAXHUB_API_KEY), network access (api.aconfig.cn), and shell command execution (curl) which are inherent to its API client function. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or malicious intent was identified.

medium_findings.title: Dangerous Combination of Risk Factors
medium_findings.description: The skill combines external command execution (curl), network access to external API endpoints, and environment variable access for API credentials. While necessary for the skill's function as an API client, this combination could be exploited if the skill were repurposed to exfiltrate credentials or make unauthorized network requests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern combination confirmed but contextual analysis shows legitimate API client behavior

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Credentials
low_findings.description: The skill accesses MAXHUB_API_KEY from the execution environment. This credential could potentially appear in error messages, command history, or debug output. The skill documentation explicitly instructs to keep API key values out of output which mitigates but does not eliminate the risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed env_access pattern for a legitimate authentication credential

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation Files
low_findings.description: The skill documentation contains hardcoded references to www.aconfig.cn across multiple files. These are legitimate API endpoint URLs for the MaxHub service and do not pose a security risk, but users should verify the service is trusted before configuring the API key.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs confirmed but all point to the documented API service provider

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Commands in Reference Documentation
low_findings.description: The reference files contain extensive Ruby/shell backtick command examples demonstrating curl usage for API calls. These are documentation patterns showing how to call the API and are not executed unless explicitly invoked by the AI assistant. The curl commands are necessary for the skill to function as an API client.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick patterns confirmed but are documentation examples only, not executed code

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution via backtick syntax
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses backtick shell execution patterns in documentation to invoke curl commands for API requests. The AI assistant may dynamically construct these commands based on user input, which creates a potential command injection surface if parameters are not properly validated.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Exposure via Environment Variable
dangerous_patterns.description: The MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable is read and used in shell commands (curl Authorization header). If command execution is logged or commands are echoed, the API key could be exposed. The skill mitigates this by instructing to keep API key values out of output.

content.user_title: Query and analyze YouTube data with MaxHub API
content.value_statement: Content creators and marketers struggle to efficiently gather YouTube video data, channel statistics, and audience insights. This skill provides a structured API interface to query YouTube data through the MaxHub platform, enabling automated video research, competitor analysis, and content planning.
actual_capabilities: Query video details including title, description, view count, likes, and publish date
Search YouTube channels and retrieve channel statistics and video lists
Fetch video comments with pagination and reply threads
Retrieve video subtitles and captions in multiple languages
Search for videos by keyword with filters for region and language
Get trending videos and related video recommendations
limitations: Requires a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY from aconfig.cn to function
Data accuracy depends on the third-party MaxHub API provider
Rate limits may apply on the API service for high-volume queries
Live stream and premiere data coverage may be limited

use_cases.title: Competitor channel analysis for content creators
use_cases.description: Search and analyze competitor YouTube channels to understand their content strategy, upload frequency, and audience engagement metrics.

use_cases.title: Video SEO research for marketers
use_cases.description: Research top-ranking videos for target keywords, analyze title patterns, tags, and descriptions to optimize video search performance.

use_cases.title: Comment sentiment analysis for brand monitoring
use_cases.description: Collect and analyze YouTube video comments to understand audience sentiment, identify feedback patterns, and track brand perception.

prompt_templates.title: Look up basic video information
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the video details for YouTube video ID dQw4w9WgXcQ. Show me the title, view count, likes, and publish date.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user retrieving a single video's metadata

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a YouTube channel
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the YouTube channel 'TEDx Talks' and get their channel information. Then fetch their most recent 10 videos and give me a summary of their content, posting frequency, and average view counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Content researcher analyzing a channel's performance

prompt_templates.title: Search and compare videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube for 'machine learning tutorial 2026' and show me the top 10 results ranked by views. For each result, include the title, channel name, view count, and publish date. Sort by view count descending.
prompt_templates.scenario: Marketer researching popular content in a niche

prompt_templates.title: Deep dive channel comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the YouTube channels 'MrBeast' and 'PewDiePie'. For each channel, get their channel info, latest 10 videos, and top comments from their most viewed video. Create a comparison table showing subscriber count, total video count, average views, average likes, and upload frequency. Then provide strategic insights about their content approaches.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user performing competitive analysis across two channels

output_examples.input: Get me the details for YouTube video dQw4w9WgXcQ
output: Title: Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up
1. 'Perfect Pasta Every Time' - Tasty (2.3M views, 3 days ago)
2. 'How to Knife Like a Chef' - Gordon Ramsay (5.1M views, 1 week ago)
3. '30 Minute Meal Prep' - Binging with Babish (1.8M views, 2 weeks ago)
Top channels in results: Tasty, Gordon Ramsay, Binging with Babish, Food Wishes

best_practices: Configure the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable before making any queries to avoid authentication errors
Run independent queries in parallel when collecting data from multiple endpoints to improve response time
Use pagination tokens when fetching large result sets to ensure complete data collection
anti_patterns: Do not embed API keys directly in prompts or command arguments as they may be logged or exposed
Do not make sequential API calls when data is independent; use parallel execution for better performance
Do not ignore error responses like 429 (rate limit) or 403 (permission denied); handle them gracefully

faq.question: What API key do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a MAXHUB_API_KEY from www.aconfig.cn. Register an account, create an API key in the dashboard, and set it as an environment variable or through your tool's configuration.

faq.question: Which AI tools are compatible with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It supports both English and Chinese language interactions.

faq.question: What YouTube data can I query with this skill?
faq.answer: You can query video details, channel information, search results, comments with replies, subtitles and captions, trending videos, and Shorts data through both Web and V2 API versions.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Chinese language queries?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill fully supports both Chinese and English. Responses are automatically formatted in the user's detected language, including number formatting (Chinese uses wan/yi, English uses K/M/B).

faq.question: Is the YouTube data provided in real time?
faq.answer: Data is fetched through the MaxHub API which provides near real-time data. However, some metrics like view counts may have a short delay based on YouTube's API caching.

faq.question: How do I handle API errors and rate limits?
faq.answer: The skill includes error handling for common HTTP codes: 401 for invalid API key, 403 for permission issues, 429 for rate limits, and 502 for server errors. Follow retry instructions from the error response.
